description: Honda's answer to the Royal Enfield Classic 350. The CB350 brings Honda's refined engineering to the retro segment. It thumps, it looks classic, and it runs like a Honda. For riders who want the retro experience without the Royal Enfield quirks.
pros: Refined engine with smooth power delivery
cons: Does not have the Royal Enfield character
Price is higher than the Classic 350
Design is retro but not as iconic
Weight is noticeable at low speeds

common_problems.problem: Slight engine noise at cold start
common_problems.cause: Normal for air-cooled engines, oil takes time to circulate
common_problems.solution: Let the engine warm up for 30 seconds before riding. Use recommended oil grade.

common_problems.cause: Indian climate is harsh on chrome finishes
common_problems.solution: Regular cleaning and chrome polish. Use bike cover when parked outdoors.

common_problems.cause: Switch mechanism wear with frequent use
common_problems.solution: Tighten or replace switch assembly at service. Common on Honda bikes.

buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories fit perfectly but cost more. For the CB350, aftermarket offers good value for non-critical items like phone mounts and bike covers. For crash protection, go OEM or from a trusted brand.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
Invest in quality riding gear — it protects you across every ride
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
